metadata.description: Synthesized findings NOT captured in network-map.json, launch-history.json, or STRATEGY.md. Top-level sections: profit_extraction_map (long-standing), extracted_findings_2026_04_26 (archive/raw-results sweep), extracted_findings_data_results_2026_04_27 (data/results/ sweep).

profit_extraction_map.description: Seven routes for extracting profits from the network.

route_a.name: Collection Wallet Route
route_a.flow: Deployers (L4-L10) + Ed4UGBWK + Bundles + Hub + Eggsheeran + JETNUT → Collection Wallet (Bra1HUNK) → Token Millionaire (49mvMufi, Fireblocks)

route_b.name: Secondary Aggregator Route
route_b.flow: OG Deployer + Hub + Bundles 1/3/5 + 6UrYwo9F relay → 2q8nSJgC → Token Millionaire (49mvMufi, Fireblocks)

route_c.name: Fireblocks Passthrough Route
route_c.flow: L7 Deployer ($31.6K) + Bundles 1/3/5/6 + Hub ($1.8K) → 9exPdTUV (Fireblocks passthrough) → 9cDDJ5g2 (Fireblocks Custody shared wallet). On-chain trail ends here.
route_c.note: Route C terminates at Fireblocks Custody (9cDDJ5g2), a shared infrastructure wallet (isUserAddress=false). Prior claim that it feeds into Route B via 2q8nSJgC is UNVERIFIABLE on-chain. The $49K may reach Token Millionaire through internal Fireblocks processes, but we cannot trace it.

route_d.name: Direct Coinbase Deposit 2
route_d.flow: Hub ($5.2K) + l9_funder ($3.1K) + jetnut_network ($2.7K) + Bundle 5 ($2.5K) + OG ($1.1K) + Bundles 2/3 → BR1HiYtc (Coinbase Deposit 2) → Coinbase hot wallets

route_e.name: Primary Coinbase Cashout
route_e.flow: Rollbit Treasury ($235K winnings) + Hub ($13.8K) + OG ($11.3K) + L4 ($4.9K) + Bundles 2/5/6 ($5.6K) + Eggsheeran ($1.8K) + 9cDDJ5g2 ($510) → coinbase_deposit (J6YUyB4P) → CB1-CB10 ($265K+)
route_e.note: Largest single cashout route. Rollbit winnings ($235K) dominate inflows.

route_f.name: Rollbit Gambling Cycle
route_f.flow: CB1-CB10 ($184K) + L8 ($4.3K) + profit_pass_2 ($5.9K) + Bundles + deployers → rollbit_deposit (RB3dQF6T) → Rollbit Treasury ($233K)
route_f.note: Money cycles: network profits → Coinbase cashout → Coinbase sends to rollbit_deposit → Rollbit gambling.

route_g.name: USDC Conversion + Binance
route_g.flow: OG/Eggsheeran/L8/LFS/Bundle 4/l9_funder → cold_usdc_2 (EAcUbdoi) → SOL-USDC swaps + 7RLD6F9S (Fireblocks, $4K) + yNanvu8H ($2.2K USDC). Also: OG/L7 → routes_binance → Binance Deposit Fx7gAJpk ($1K) + 7RLD6F9S ($2.6K)
route_g.volume: $6.6K to 7RLD6F9S Fireblocks + $2.2K USDC to yNanvu8H + $1K to Binance

endpoint.token_millionaire: 49mvMufixEbUMzZEUVAnbQ8hLLuEWy789XfKUNTz7wCv — Fireblocks Custody (Arkham). Sends $45.3M to Large Funding Source. Registry verdict (2026-03-31, not revalidated): does not appear in any traced deployer-network outbound chain in the reviewed window.
endpoint.total_extracted: $400K+ confirmed. Routes E and F overlap significantly — Rollbit winnings flow to Coinbase, Coinbase funds flow back to Rollbit.

profit_extraction_map.fireblocks_caveat: Arkham labels many wallets 'Fireblocks Custody' on Solana — broad label covering any wallet interacting with Fireblocks infrastructure. Does NOT mean same entity.

extracted_findings_2026_04_26.description: Findings extracted during 2026-04-26 cleanup pass before deleting source files in archive/raw-results/ (39 files) and archive/handoffs/ (5 SESSION_HANDOFF_*.md). Registry-shape entries kept here so cleanup didn't drop signal. Pending registry promotion via reviewed patch.

novel_wallets_pending_registry_review.source: Helius getWalletIdentity (2026-04-25); also queued in current-wallet-review-scope.json as proposed_patch_outline
novel_wallets_pending_registry_review.evidence: Helius identity = Bitstamp Hot Wallet. ~44,987 SOL balance. Pre-activated MP1 candidate id 148 (6uYzo6ZRYehusYyaaM8XbZE11da98hyaryBtDKVyB8Bb) with 0.135 SOL 11 minutes before MoonPay funding.
Receives $7.5K from registry's ChcEbZW2 across 9 transactions; largest outflow target. Funded by 2ZrTHH1SyvwLPsypKazVUWQ2DjdxZi9xg4XjqkcBe253.
Funded by BmFdpraQhkiDQE6SnfG5omcA1VwzqfXrwtNYBwWTymy6 (KuCoin Hot Wallet 2). Sends $701 to GoonPump (231fshU82vSSeyytyCZJUEUG8Ti3UAgnDSoNFGTETCuK).
Nansen 'Distributor' label. Funded by u6PJ8DtQuPFnfmwHbGFULQ4u4EgjDiyYKjVEsynXq2w (Nansen 'Gate: Wallet'). Sends $1,249 to GoonPump.
Rollbit-prefix vanity. Arkham returns 10 batch-tx hashes shared with sibling RB2Yz3VS. Registry mentions Rollbit batch-tx anomaly in rollbit_deposit notes but doesn't carry this address.
Same anomaly cluster as RB5KKB7h — identical 10 batch-tx hashes.
Solana .sol domain 'lucky🍀flip🎰gg.sol' / 'casino🎲flip🎰gg.sol' (Nansen). Bulk dust-spammer (1 lamport pings) to deployer-network and non-network wallets.

novel_wallets_pending_registry_review.proposed_label: flip-gg.sol gambling-domain dust spammer (companion of AFXigaYu)

novel_wallets_pending_registry_review.evidence: Every occurrence is direction=in, amount=1 lamport SOL. Provider enrichment in correct-cashout-profile.json labels source as 'Spam'. Operational pair with FLipG5QHjZe1H12f6rr5LCnrmqjhwuBTBp78GwzxnwkR. See negative_findings_preserved for evidence-scope ruling out deployer-signal interpretation.

novel_wallets_pending_registry_review.proposed_label: flip-gg.sol gambling dust spammer (companion of FLipgewP)
novel_wallets_pending_registry_review.source: archive/raw-results/* — 70+ occurrences across same 10 raw-result files
novel_wallets_pending_registry_review.evidence: Same pattern as FLipgewP. Same provider 'Spam' label.

negative_findings_preserved.description: Interpretive findings worth preserving so future passes don't re-investigate.
negative_findings_preserved.flip_prefix_pair_is_not_deployer_signal: FLipgewPwNeqvwPFW3CvMTLpHTvuX7BQoXC6xhrWiCR3 + FLipG5QHjZe1H12f6rr5LCnrmqjhwuBTBp78GwzxnwkR were initially considered as parallel to the 2Zi-prefix vanity cluster. Evidence rules that out: (a) bulk-fanout to many unrelated wallets including out-of-network targets (e.g. 9exPdTUV Fireblocks passthrough); (b) provider-enrichment labels source as 'Spam'; (c) infrastructure for the flip-gg.sol gambling/casino dusting service — companion vanity wallets AFXigaYu and BMq4XUa3 carry Nansen .sol domain labels for casino🎲flip🎰gg.sol etc. Verdict: gambling-domain dust-spammer, not deployer signal.
negative_findings_preserved.odin_pinger_family_is_generic_monitoring: Per SESSION_HANDOFF_2026-04-25.md analytical finding 1: odin-prefix family (8+ siblings observed) appears to be a generic fresh-wallet monitoring service (likely MEV/sniper). Earlier CoinSpot-batch interpretation that odin pings were a deployer signal is materially weakened by direct evidence: odin pingers were observed pinging confirmed memecoin traders within hours of MoonPay funding. HYM-prefix tradecraft on L10 deployer (preserved in current-wallet-review-scope.json) remains the only confirmed deployer-network vanity signal beyond 2Zi.
negative_findings_preserved.tri_prefix_is_broadcast_pinger: TriQKRfbS1v63hvHpLWNnb51VL9GvVeotTYYXSfUDHb attempted 1-lamport pings to 20+ wallets in a single failed multi-target tx — broadcaster behavior, not targeted-operator behavior. Low signal.

suggestive_leads_low_confidence.context: Arkham 'J5XGHm on Pump.fun' (Pump.fun deployer alias), 317.9 SOL balance. Direct counterparty in RXRP repump trace.
Distinct address from registry's FKjuwJzHn4hqSfarMMsKjzsNUzEuWzzDM4ZcqJzuATqu. Profile claims funding-chain inbound but Helius shows 0 sigs / 0 transfers / 0 balance.
suggestive_leads_low_confidence.next_step: Verify on-chain — likely transcription error (CLAUDE.md primitive-integrity past-incidents flagged FKjuw prefix specifically).

suggestive_leads_low_confidence.context: Arkham 'daddyisback on Pump.fun'. Single-tx sender to registry's 21wG4F3Z (binance_deposit_generic, not_network).
suggestive_leads_low_confidence.next_step: Likely external retail. Drop unless other links surface.

evidence_pointers_kept_in_data_results.description: Handoff narratives were synthesis. The underlying evidence files remain in data/results/ and are the source-of-truth for these findings.
evidence_pointers_kept_in_data_results.susye_mass_arming_l11_evidence: data/results/figg-l11-armed-check-2026-04-25.json — SUSYE deployer 2Zizao3xpJGsoUFdvTJjYL8LijtFxR9v36cNBeDJJVSz funded 9 wallets in a 12-min window 2026-04-22 12:59-13:11 UTC, each bracketed by 2Zi-prefix dust pings (decisive H1 evidence for SUSYE/DmA9 half). Tier-1 L11 candidate list lives in this file.
evidence_pointers_kept_in_data_results.operator_known_dev_wallets_cross_ref: data/results/operator-known-dev-wallets-2026-04-25.json — 33 operator-supplied dev wallets, 21 already in registry, 12 net-new, 1 dropped (HV1KXxWFaSeriyFvXyx48FqG9BoFbfinB8njCJonqP7K = OKX DEX Router Authority, not a wallet). Zero overlap with Figg-cluster member set.
evidence_pointers_kept_in_data_results.figg_phase_d_arkham_attribution: data/results/figg-phase-d-attribution-extracted-2026-04-25.json — 77-address Arkham label sweep. Confirmed labels: 2eVVdqWN4t7umzXGxJ21uTpB5WrUyyQMYnkq6UHtg3ba + 83Nf4kNbeaRSGwjYyStK4F3ctmH3v5Yar2GmWv9fJqf6 = Rainbet Deposit; 5HQZd9ovzAF1TLnHRAq1zcSnXC9HAp3EwhoxMHvo8rxB = Rainbet Hot Wallet; 2wGZCehkDBMDPLMNXJjFf7kjAm46MJV3t3htmGVa4S16 = Kraken Deposit; 43PcTMd37rBVECXqPjqSHBaM2JMPbiFvpF2Eei8u3LyU = Coinbase Deposit; 784bxhz6xUA88Rk6jb1aTU7FjjkoxAmASsrSDEry2Zvw = userEntity 'Panther'. Pending registry-note updates.
evidence_pointers_kept_in_data_results.h1_vs_h2_session_verdict: 2026-04-25 session verdict: ~70-75% H1 (sniper network is deployer-owned silo). SUSYE/DmA9 half is unambiguously dev (mass-arming + dust signaling cannot be explained under H2). Figg half H1-leaning (shared Bloom config + same on-ramp MP2 + same-slot synchronized launches at L10) but cashout-terminus convergence not yet exhaustively traced. To close to >90% H1: any Figg-cluster terminus reaching Bra1HUNK / 49mvMufi / J6YUyB4P (Coinbase deposit) or shared Fireblocks deposit.
evidence_pointers_kept_in_data_results.7dkvxgj_open_question: data/results/figg-phase-d-attribution-2026-04-25.json — 7DkvxGJbv9qiNGqqE7VZ5jdg6SLDgBtem7NgaiPmnrzA Arkham label '7dkvxg on Pump.fun' with 3 Created Tokens: HEZdEXw8rirh9NhY9aqyDC3ExGzMs2hQRTRpASD8pump, zjZfVkorGxFT6TyHSv2a34SK9TuEEoh4az34jfUpump, CcDdm6YQX5Z6mrq68siqepNUSTjRWQ3McKR89aGFXray. Decisive H1/H2 test deferred: if any block-0 buyer of these 3 tokens is in Figg-cluster or known deployer-network, that's H1-decisive.

follow_up_destinations_unprofiled.context: Received 16.767 SOL from RXRP-chain candidate id 86 (FTX-US-insider chain).
Received 19.9 SOL from MP1 candidate id 77.

infrastructure_attribution_preserved_pre.description: Attribution-grade endpoint addresses that were only documented in deleted source files. Lower-priority than novel_wallets, but worth preserving so future passes don't re-derive.

Source: data/results/figg-hop3-trace.json (deleted in commit da64bafc), referenced from SESSION_HANDOFF_2026-04-25_PART3_7DK_FIREBLOCKS.md §4.2 B5 + §6.3 (also being deleted in this pass).

addresses.volume: $20M (handoff was internally inconsistent on USDT vs USDC; preserve dollar volume only)

fireblocks_9cdd_top_outflow_terminuses.next_step: Probe each for Arkham labels — may resolve as Fireblocks-internal trading routes vs specific exchange deposits. Decisive for the H1/H2 cashout-terminus convergence test.

ed4ugbwk_profit_pass_1_cashout_endpoints.context: Cashout terminus addresses for the profit_pass_1 → Ed4UGBWK → exchange-deposit chain. Source: archive/raw-results/suspect-address-verification.json + infra-verify.json (both deleted in this pass). Registry's profit_pass_1 entry describes '$247K out, 711 txs' but does not enumerate destinations — these 13 endpoints are the destinations. Labels are Nansen-attributed.
ed4ugbwk_profit_pass_1_cashout_endpoints.framing: Extends profit_extraction_map. Routes A/D/E/F documented Coinbase/Rollbit/Binance/Fireblocks. Stake.com / Shuffle.com / Bitvavo / Bitgo / KuCoin are net-new endpoint attribution at the profit_pass_1 → Ed4UGBWK cashout layer.

extracted_findings_data_results_2026_04_.description: Findings extracted during 2026-04-27 cleanup pass before deleting 18 demoted files in data/results/. Six KEEP files retained (figg-l11-armed-check, figg-phase-d-attribution, figg-phase-d-attribution-extracted, figg-phase-d-new-members, operator-known-dev-wallets, investigation-notes itself). All addresses cross-checked verbatim against network-map.json + extracted_findings_2026_04_26 — only entries absent from both are preserved here.

novel_infrastructure_5my_cluster_pending.description: USDC→SOL pre-staging system that bypasses the existing MoonPay/CoinSpot monitor sieves. Source: data/results/l11-deploy-watchlist-2026-04-25.json. Self-labeled in source as 'NEW — propose registry add'. Highest-confidence novel infrastructure in the cleanup. Funded chain: registry's L9 Side Funder (52eC8Uy5eFkwpGbDbXp1FoarxkR8MonwUvpm2WT9ni5B) → 5MytJocz aggregator → fresh staged wallets via daily ~700 USDC → ~8 SOL OKX swap pattern (observed Apr 18-23).

addresses.evidence: Daily ~700 USDC OKX swap → ~8 SOL → fresh wallet pattern observed Apr 18-23. Funded J1Abcje L11 Tier-S candidate on Apr 21.
50.12 SOL reserve. Pings 5MyJWED splitter and recipients in matched-pair coordination patterns. Not the deploy wallet itself.
Multi-recipient splitter for treasury distribution. Tag-coordination wallet.

novel_infrastructure_5my_cluster_pending.monitor_implication: USDC-funded staging chain entirely bypasses both MoonPay AND CoinSpot sieves. Recommend post-L11 monitor work: add USDC outflows from L9 Side Funder + 5MytJocz as monitor source events. Add 7qN aggregator + 32Db relay outflow detection. (One referenced source 'FncazAs6' is truncated in the source file and is not preserved here.)

l11_pre_launch_candidate_set_post_mortem.description: L11 Nukex launched 2026-04-25; this is the pre-launch candidate snapshot taken at 2026-04-25T21:11:00Z (~49 min before launch window close). Preserved as post-mortem evidence — which candidates fired vs. went stale informs L12 sieve calibration. Source: data/results/l11-deploy-watchlist-2026-04-25.json. Source file also names 5 'drained sibling pre-staged wallets' (8CvuX95R, 37KoU8ys, AfAtbk3A, 7DPDLZDy, 2pJkaJQL) using 8-char prefixes only — full addresses are not in source so are not preserved here.

tier_s_primed_deploy_ready.monitor_status_at_snapshot: NOT_MONITORED at snapshot time; operator confirmed adding to whitelist
tier_s_primed_deploy_ready.verdict_at_snapshot: Highest-confidence L11 candidate at the time. Pre-staged 4 days ahead. Sole survivor of 6 sibling pre-staged wallets.
Best-fit MoonPay candidate at snapshot — 13.5h pre-window, textbook timing, untouched.
Strong MP2 candidate at snapshot — 25h pre-window edge of historical max.
Hidden L11 candidate, MP2 routed through aggregator. Untouched, primed.

monitor_coverage_gaps_for_l12: MoonPay sieve only catches direct MP→fresh wallets. 2-hop chains bypass: 4 confirmed at L11 snapshot (CrCugmcJ→OTC, 2UzMoao3→arb bot, DD2YEZf→v8NL, 32Db8JVK→Axiom bot).
USDC-funded staging chain entirely bypasses both MoonPay AND CoinSpot sieves. L9 Side Funder + 5MytJocz operate via OKX USDC→SOL swaps. Not surfaced by current monitor.
Recommend post-L11: add USDC outflows from L9 Side Funder + 5MytJocz as monitor source events; add 7qN aggregator + 32Db relay outflow detection.

xaic_asset_trace_extracted.description: Complete asset-disposition trace of 326,752,146 XAIC tokens. XAIC is the L10 launch token (mint KfByHk48ecitUq8gXji2vr9smmRJKtqJwGAh2E9pump): L10 Deployer 2mZzsVKNz1zJKRnLz2X74qGPMcSNGCkRM1U5BShsVMVB transferred 100% to OG Deployer 37XxihfsTW1EFSJJherWFRFWcAFhj4KQ66cXHiegSKg2 at +44 seconds post-deploy, and OG Deployer is the source-wallet of this disposition trace. Source: data/results/xaic-asset-trace.json. Hop-1 recipients are all already in registry; hop-2/hop-3/SOL-beneficiaries are novel here. 99.9985% sold via pumpswap pool (EDrEnp4twH2Bjzp28hdrrWPdN9sMyRCCMWCkXfnPc8yH); 0% reached labeled exchange. One paired beneficiary is recorded with only an 8-character prefix in source — not preserved verbatim here.

xaic_token_infrastructure.launcher_finding: XAIC was deployed via a non-standard custom launcher program (DeJBGdMFa1uynnnKiwrVioatTuHmNLpyFKnmB5kaFdzQ), NOT pump.fun's main deploy program (6EF8rrecthR5Dkzon8Nwu78hRvfCKubJ14M5uBEwF6P). The same custom launcher was also used for ATA-creation-plus-transfer to hop-1 recipient 8fCxXEEqdv8a8CZ5QvLm6xzE45qYGZ3iCcRqmLLhDPms and hop-3 recipient 311mKBYRBYSqjEiMWaAvvyEZ99X4vhZrD2GSmTj4uqDY.

hop2_recipients.disposition: all sold (2 txs); ALSO operator-tagged dev wallet at L6/L7/L9 per kept file operator-known-dev-wallets-2026-04-25.json (not_in_registry list)

all sold (2 txs); SOL proceeds went to beneficiary YAh1vvZPnuCjZ354LF8ria76cJaSEPvPyPYuRoxVkuH under the delegate pattern; ALSO operator-tagged dev wallet at L7 per kept file operator-known-dev-wallets-2026-04-25.json (not_in_registry list)
active wallet (835 lifetime sigs); gross-sold 6.8M after round-trips; ATA closed
active wallet (251 lifetime sigs); all 4M sold; ATA closed

hop3_recipients.disposition: all sold via Jupiter-v6 → pumpswap 2m12s after receipt

sol_beneficiary_wallets_delegate_pattern.description: Wallets that received SOL from third-party-signed XAIC sells under delegate/authority — distinct from the XAIC-holder owner accounts. Owner-account SOL deltas understate realized proceeds because proceeds went to these beneficiaries instead. Several appear in paired-symmetric pumpswap-signature patterns (each member of a pair receives half of the realized SOL).

addresses.from: CzE9 sells (paired with a counter-account that is recorded with only an 8-character prefix in source)

interpretive_findings.no_cex_exit: Helius batch identity for all 37 traced addresses (hop1 + hop2 + hop3 + SOL-beneficiaries + source + launcher) returned type=unknown. No traced wallet is a Helius-labeled CEX deposit, bridge, or labeled protocol destination. Everything terminal is the pumpswap AMM pool. Strong negative finding: XAIC sale proceeds did NOT exit to a labeled exchange in the traced window.
interpretive_findings.delegate_authority_pattern: Many hop-1 and hop-2 wallets had XAIC sold by third-party signers under delegate/authority, with SOL landing in beneficiary wallets distinct from the XAIC holder. Owner-account native-SOL deltas therefore understate realized proceeds. Lower-bound total realized SOL across owner accounts ~462 SOL; true total is higher once delegate beneficiaries are counted.
interpretive_findings.non_pump_fun_launcher: XAIC was deployed via non-pump.fun custom launcher DeJBGdMFa1uynnnKiwrVioatTuHmNLpyFKnmB5kaFdzQ — not the pump.fun main deploy program (6EF8rrecthR5Dkzon8Nwu78hRvfCKubJ14M5uBEwF6P). Worth flagging in any future deploy-program inventory.

rxrp_priority_counterparties_extracted.description: Specific instance addresses for the priority counterparties review of RXRP-investigation buyers. Source: data/results/rxrp-priority-counterparties-review-summary-2026-04-24.json. Investigation-notes already records the family-level downgrade verdict in negative_findings_preserved.odin_pinger_family_is_generic_monitoring; these addresses are the specific named instances behind that verdict, plus generic dust/tip/ATA infrastructure surfaced in the same review.

odin_prefix_named_instances.context: odin-prefix sibling, signer in 5/5 + fee payer in 4/5 most-recent parsed txs at review time. Direct counterparty to canonical RXRP wallet. Sent 8.709 SOL to odinZ in a bounded Arkham window. 0 SOL balance at review (drained).
odin_prefix_named_instances.interpretation: Concrete instance of the generic-monitoring odin family. The medium control_confidence + drained-but-active pattern is worth watching for re-arm; this is the actionable subset of the odin family.

odin_prefix_named_instances.context: Nansen label 'valhalla.sol'. Received 8.709 SOL from odin8 in bounded Arkham rows. Funded by odinFq in recent txs. 3.232 SOL at review.
odin_prefix_named_instances.interpretation: Aggregator/sink for odin family. Same generic-monitoring verdict applies.

odin_prefix_named_instances.context: Active odin-family source/fee-payer. Repeated ~0.4 SOL chunk-funding to odinZ. 10.869 SOL at review.

generic_dust_and_tip_infrastructure.context: Signer + fee payer in 5/5 recent parsed txs. 100 one-lamport outbound system transfers in 5 txs. 0.246 SOL at review. Functionally similar to TriQKRfb (already in notes) but distinct address.
Helius identity = 'Temporal Tip Account 16'. Inbound-only. 0.095 SOL at review.
Helius identity = 'Temporal Tip Account 2'. Inbound-only. 0.066 SOL at review.

subject_owned_wsol_swap_atas.context: Confirmed via Arkham ownerAddress. WSOL plumbing for Jupiter/Phantom. 0 SOL.
subject_owned_wsol_swap_atas.interpretation: Token account, not a wallet. Recorded so future Nansen counterparty profiles for AZ57WTN don't re-investigate this address as a wallet.

subject_owned_wsol_swap_atas.context: Confirmed via Arkham ownerAddress. WSOL plumbing for pump.fun. 0 SOL.

resolved_prior_lead_j5xghm.prior_status_in_2026_04_26_section: suggestive_leads_low_confidence with next_step 'Layer-1/Layer-2 profiling before promotion' under the assumption it was an Arkham-labeled 'J5XGHm on Pump.fun' deployer alias.
resolved_prior_lead_j5xghm.resolution_from_data_results_review: rxrp-priority-counterparties-review-summary-2026-04-24.json classifies as terminal_fees_account with provider label 'Helius: Terminal (formerly Padre) Fees'. 317.9 SOL balance is consistent with a fees-collection account, not a deployer wallet. Helius identity beats Arkham label here.
resolved_prior_lead_j5xghm.resolved_verdict: External infrastructure (Terminal/Padre Fees), do_not_monitor_as_network_wallet despite large balance. Removes the 'next_step: profile' lead from the prior section.

5my_to_jetnut_to_wfxrp_observation.description: On L11 day 2026-04-25, registry's Jetnut Network wallet FSbvLdrK1FuWJSNVfyguDQgvt93Zk92KnGxxSHoFjAyE (20.25 SOL at 21:04 UTC snapshot) bought 1.93M + 415K WFXRP (an OG-Deployer-created sister token) and bundled to 3 recipients via the 5MytJoczvqkiYPjTrvviqCjudw46ZwTLaLkQsVUB4irj feed. Source: data/results/l11-deploy-watchlist-2026-04-25.json.
5my_to_jetnut_to_wfxrp_observation.implication: Connects the new 5My infrastructure cluster (in this section's novel_infrastructure_5my_cluster_pending_registry_review) to the existing Jetnut Network registry entry via a coordinated WFXRP buy + bundle. Strengthens the 5My-cluster registry-promotion case: 5My is not just a USDC-aggregation hub but is also feeding deployer-network bundle activity on launch days.

negative_findings_preserved_2026_04_27.rxrp_buyers_no_fresh_load_in_5tx_windows: 5-tx parsed windows for the 6 highest-balance RXRP buyer wallets (7iVCXQn4, 7JCe3GHw, 54Pz1e35, 7cthuERB, AZ57WTN, BvYi1ZV9 — all in registry) show active trading or setup churn, not fresh-load / consolidation / deployer-prep transfer patterns. Provider coverage was limited (Nansen 422 on 3 highest-balance wallets; Arkham transfer rows capped at 10 with sample_assessment=partial_only). Source operational guidance: treat as pre-deploy monitor surfaces first, copy-trade value second; do not downshift the canonical registry verdicts from this narrow-window observation. Source: rxrp-full-investigation-summary-2026-04-24.json + wallet-review-rxrp-repump-* trio.
negative_findings_preserved_2026_04_27.rxrp_repump_funder_anchors_are_known_cex: rxrp-repump-screen-results.json identified 2 shared funders for repump-bot pairs: G2YxRa6wt1qePMwfJzdXZG62ej4qaTC7YURzuh2Lwd3t (ChangeNOW Hot Wallet 2) and ASTyfSima4LLAdDgoFGkgqoKowG1LZFDr9fAQrg7iaJZ (MEXC Exchange). Both are known-CEX hot wallets per provider labels — not deployer-network signal. Pairs: B4sW2zUw + 4nNAJVQj share ChangeNOW; 4SsCN1i5 + F8XieEYF share MEXC.
negative_findings_preserved_2026_04_27.cross_reference_l1_l10_recurrence: data/results/cross-reference-report.json (2026-03-31) ran L1-L10 early-buyer recurrence analysis across 292 unique wallets, surfaced 56 recurring. Findings already absorbed into network-map.json registry roles (known_insider, known_infra, network_connected, monitoring). All 56 recurring wallets present in registry — no novel addresses. Methodology pointer: this was the canonical artifact for 'wallet X appears as early buyer in N launches'.
